TextENGNew: Me and Satoshi... what about us?
Everything I'd done today, thinking it was for the best, had been nothing more than an imitation of Satoshi.
Then Satoshi too... had he been in the same situation as me...?
Did the friends he'd spent such happy days with suddenly change... and start coming for his life for no reason (...or only for reasons I failed to notice...)?
And like me, did he agonize himself to the limit, take up a bat for self-defense, and carry it around all the time under the pretense of practice swings...?
And then one day, all of a sudden...
He transferred away.
changeReason: Original 'He transferred.' (in quotes as dialogue) vs new 'He transferred away.' (no quotes, narrative). JPN uses quotation marks, and 転校しちゃったの has a casual/regretful tone.

TextENGNew: Something cold and numbing flowed through the blood in my whole body.
From nearest the heart outward, it coursed through me, freezing everything evenly from the crown of my head to the tips of my toes...
What do you mean, transferred? What do you mean, transferred? Is Satoshi even there where he transferred to?
Will he at least understand me?
Will he tell me everything about why this happened?
More importantly, where the hell did he transfer to?!
...What do you mean, transferred? What do you mean...?
Before I knew it, I was standing in front of my own door.
The cool doorknob felt heavy. ...No one's home?
It wasn't that unusual, so I rummaged in my pocket and pulled out the key with the seal keychain.
The instant I stepped into the entryway and started to take off my shoes, my spine froze.
Because someone had come in through the front door pressed right up against me.
Like a classmate messing around at school... that thing stood there, flush against my back...
No way... This has to be my imagination...
At this kind of point-blank distance, there's no way anyone could follow me all the way into the house without me sensing them. Common sense says that's impossible...
But without any doubt, it was there behind me.
...Hey, hey, Keiichi.
...If it's behind you, how do you even know it's there...?!
...Because... I heard the sound of hair swaying.
A sound like that can't exist. That's just the sense of someone there.
changeReason: Original 'There's no other reason... I'd hear that sound. That was the presence.' vs new 'A sound like that can't exist. That's just the sense of someone there.' Different logical structure - original says the sound proves the presence; new denies the sound's existence.

TextENGNew: ...Because... I heard the sound of someone blinking.
A sound like that can't exist. Maebara Keiichi!
changeReason: Original 'Keiichi Maebara, there's no way you could hear that!' vs new 'A sound like that can't exist. Maebara Keiichi!' Name order differs (Western vs Japanese) and the logic differs.

TextENGNew: Every instinctive sense, the ones closest to the wild, warned me of a presence behind me, while my most rational common sense denied it as imagination.
I clawed at the eerie image in my mind, telling myself this was all in my head, that no one was behind me.
But at the same time, if no one was there, then what was this thing that was there behind me now...? That eeriness crawled up my spine too.
Wouldn't it be better... if someone really were there...?
If... you turned around and nobody was there... could you accept that...?
If I just turned my neck and looked behind me, every question would be answered.
...But I wasn't brave enough... to do something that simple...
Right... I'll try calling out... The person behind me... might answer.
My thoughts had wandered so far that this was what they proposed: any method was fine, as long as I didn't have to turn around.
...If I'd been calm... I would've realized that solved nothing at all...
Wh-... who is it...?
changeReason: Original has quotes (dialogue formatting) 'Wh... Who is it...?'; new has no quotes (internal thought). JPN doesn't have explicit quotes here.

TextENGNew: A hoarse voice I could hardly believe was mine crumbled out from deep in my throat.
It felt as though the one behind me was hesitating to answer.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ..."It felt"? There's no way I could know that!
Calm down, Keiichi, this is all in your head...!!
At that moment, I definitely heard it.
Hesitating at my question, yet trying to answer... I definitely heard the sound of a breath being drawn in.
I heard it.
I heard it.
I heard it clearly.
It was a woman.
changeReason: Original 'It was a girl' vs new 'It was a woman.' JPN 女 means woman/female; 'girl' implies younger age than 'woman.'

TextENGNew: It was a young woman.
changeReason: Original 'A young girl' vs new 'It was a young woman.' Same issue -- 女 vs girl/woman.

TextENGNew: I didn’t know who she was. But anyway, anyway...
A single grain of courage inside me, reckless courage, gave me the most primitive power of all, and the one most fitting for this situation: it made me shout.
I unleashed every ounce of strength from my lungs, from my throat, and severed every thought in my mind.
Killing every thought and emotion, I fell as if collapsing, yet twisted my body and looked back.
It was definitely there.
It was there.
Someone was there.
It was certainly there right up until I turned and cast my gaze behind me.
I fell onto my back and traced with my eyes the presence lingering in the empty air...
...No way... this thing is transparent... looks like it isn’t there... but it’s still standing here...???
With that scream, the wave of emotion I’d been holding back came flooding out all at once.
But I was remarkably calm as that emotional dam burst.
...I deftly guided the emotional surge crashing over me like a muddy torrent... and turned it into aggression.
To break through the abnormal situation right before my eyes... it was an emotion I absolutely needed.
With perfect calm, I surrendered myself to raging passion...
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: The metal bat fit in my right arm as if it were glued there.
A horizontal sweep at mid-level is the hardest to dodge... I’m sure I read that in a kendo book or something.
OoooooOOOOOOOHHHHHHH!!!!!
changeReason: Original has quotes (dialogue) 'RAaaAAaAAaAh!!!!!'; new has no quotes (internal/narrative scream). Formatting difference changes whether this is spoken aloud.

TextENGNew: A single explosive burst of spirit.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: The afterimage of a silver-glinting lump of metal scythed across the entryway from left to right!!!!
The bat slammed into the wall on the right and, with tremendous recoil, snapped its tip back.
...With perfect calm, I controlled that rebounding force and direction, and reversed it into an attack to the left.
It smashed through the shoe cabinet door.
Those two swings just now cut through empty air, but they seemed to have a major psychological effect on the enemy.
...Because I could feel impatience leaking into the air.
Attack alone wasn't enough.
...I wrenched the bat out of the shoe cabinet where it had sunk in, spun my whole body through the swing, and roared.
UOOOOOOOOOOOHHHHHH!!!
changeReason: Original has quotes 'WRAaAAAAaAAaAAAhhh!!!'; new has no quotes. Same formatting significance.

TextENGNew: My shout shook the air, adding even more destructive force to the all-out blow that followed...!!
KRAAAAAAASH!!!!
A merciless, full-power strike worthy of a one-hit kill smashed down the top panel of the shoe cabinet.
None of my attacks hit the enemy, but my raging fury definitely did.
By the time I was heaving for breath, my whole body drenched in hot sweat, that invisible enemy that was there and not there had dispersed like mist.
Once I sensed the enemy had withdrawn, I locked the front door and set the chain too.
What if it only pretended to retreat, and had already stepped into my house?!?!
I bared my aggression again and searched for a presence inside the house... but there was none.
I'd succeeded in driving it off.
...At that moment, all strength drained from my body, and a huge sigh spilled out...
The brain secretions I'd kept suppressed until just now churned together into a mess and flooded into me, a jumble of fear, accomplishment, and distrust.
Rather than deal with each of those feelings one by one, I crushed them all with the strongest feeling of all: exhaustion.
Even at that moment... I was calm.
After checking every lock in the house again, I returned to my room on the second floor and drew the curtains.
Then I stood upright, tilted my head back slightly... erased every stray thought, and cooled my mind into even deeper calm...
What on earth was that at the front door just now...?
It was definitely there.
Now that I'd calmed down like this, I thought maybe I could see it as a hallucination from my panic... but I still couldn't believe that.
Calm down, Maebara Keiichi... Be cooler. Much cooler...
changeReason: Original 'Calm down, Keiichi Maebara...' (Western name order) vs new 'Calm down, Maebara Keiichi...' (Japanese name order). Also 'Compose yourself' vs 'Be cooler. Much cooler' -- JPN もっともっとクールに means 'more and more cool.'

TextENGNew: But no matter how calmly I thought it through... what just happened wasn't any hallucination.
That was clearly a supernatural phenomenon, and there was definitely something behind me.
It wasn't that I snapped, or got confused, or saw some hallucination!
Proof? I have exactly one.
...When I asked, 'Who is it?', the one behind me drew in breath to answer. I heard that sound clearly with my own ears.
The situation I'm in is still unclear.
Either I've been swept up in the supernatural phenomenon called Oyashiro-sama's curse,
or this was set up by villagers who blindly believe in it, or imitate it.
Either way, the motive is unknown.
That roundabout method remains a mystery too.
If a human is the culprit (which would mean admitting Rena and the others are the culprits...), then solving this is still relatively easy.
Ooishi-san and the police will surely arrest them.
But... if Oyashiro-sama's curse really exists... then what?
Ooishi-san stated clearly that no such curse exists.
At the time, those words felt very reassuring... but now, with the possibility rising that no human culprit exists... they suddenly feel unreliable.
What would happen if I told Ooishi-san, just once, that this is Oyashiro-sama's curse?
...I can't imagine his reaction, but one thing is certain: the distance between us would open up all at once.
I already have so few allies, and I still can't conclude whether this is a curse or not, so doing that would gain me nothing.
It's better to keep the 'fact' of what happened at the entrance a moment ago to myself.
...I shouldn't write what just happened in the memo hidden behind the clock, either.
There is still, however slight, the possibility that this state I believe to be calm is already confusion, and I simply lashed out at the entrance...
If only that were true.
changeReason: Original 'How wonderful would that be if that was really what happened?' (elaborate) vs new 'If only that were true.' (concise). JPN そうだったらどんなにいいだろう means 'how great it would be if that were so.'

TextENGNew: I could deny Oyashiro-sama's curse.
But if I deny Oyashiro-sama's curse, that means admitting Rena and the others are the culprits.
If I deny that Rena and the others are the culprits, that means accepting Oyashiro-sama's curse...
And if I deny both... that means admitting I'm the one who's abnormal.
Three impossible choices lock each other in a deadlock, and as they mix together in a muddy whirlpool, they spin my vision round and round...
Once again, I stood up straight and tipped my head back a little, trying to cool my thoughts.
...Calm down, Keiichi.
...Only what happened is the truth.
...Stop thinking beyond that...
But... I can't help it. I keep thinking.
...If I were the one who'd gone crazy, and everything that's happened so far were just a hallucination... how wonderful that would be.
Oyashiro-sama's curse wouldn't be real, and Rena and the others would still be the best friends I've ever had...
I want to go crazy.
changeReason: Original 'I would have to be crazy' (hypothetical/conditional) vs new 'I want to go crazy' (desire). JPN 俺はおかしくなりたい means 'I want to go crazy' -- expressing a wish, which the new captures correctly.

TextENGNew: For the first time in my life, I wished for that.
The phone downstairs suddenly began ringing shrilly.
I hardly ever get calls, so I rarely pick up the phone on my own.
But my parents were out, so I had no choice.
I dragged myself out of bed and went downstairs.
Hello, Maebara speaking.
changeReason: Original has quotes (dialogue) 'Hello, this is the Maebara residence'; new has no quotes (narrative). Formatting changes character presentation.

TextENGNew: Keiichi? It's Mom.
changeReason: Original has quotes 'Keiichi? This is Mom.'; new has no quotes. Same formatting change.

TextENGNew: ...I had a bad feeling right away. I figured she was about to ask me to go shopping.
So I decided to say it first.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: What? I don't mind instant food for dinner. We still have plenty left, don't we?
changeReason: Original has quotes (dialogue); new has no quotes (narration). Changes presentation.

TextENGNew: The other day, when we went shopping as a family, we'd bought boxed cup ramen.
I wanted to buy lots of different kinds, but they said it was too expensive, so we got a dozen of my favorite giant tonkotsu ginger flavor instead.
But my parents don't seem to like heavy, rich flavors, so they won't touch them. That's why we've still got plenty in stock.
So there's no need to go shopping, right?
Keiichi, I'm not asking you to go shopping. Your father and I suddenly have to go to Tokyo for work.
changeReason: Original has quotes with 'Mommy and Daddy'; new has no quotes with 'Your father and I.' JPN お父さんとお母さん can be either. Also register/formality difference.

TextENGNew: Wh-What...?! Right now?!
It really was... completely out of the blue.
No, we've already arrived. We left around noon.
Tokyo isn't exactly a short trip from Hinamizawa. Even flooring it on the expressway takes six hours.
...Dad has a license, but he hates expressways, so they go by train. ...That takes even longer.
Keiichi, you might have noticed from listening to us, but your father's work contract isn't going very well right now.
Now that she mentioned it, I remembered my parents talking all last night about how bad business had gotten.
Your father is sensitive about that sort of thing, so if this keeps up, it'll start affecting his work.
Dad has that artist's glass-fragile sense of self, with emotions as changeable as an autumn sky. ...Or, put simply, he can't take a hit.
But... couldn't you just handle that over the phone?!
Keiichi, it's your father's work, so try to support him a little more, okay? Anyway, it's faster to meet and talk in person than over the phone. It avoids misunderstandings too.
Once she brought up work, there was nothing I, as his son, could really say.
So we won't be back until tomorrow night. Keiichi, you'll be all right on your own, won't you?
It's not like I'm gonna die.
...Keiichi, don't say things like dying so lightly. ...If something's worrying you, talk to me, okay? I think your mom can help.
Maybe she was worried, as a parent, because last night I suddenly started saying things like if I die.
...Still, it hurt knowing that even if I talked, it wouldn't solve anything.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: But I had no intention of dying.
...At least not before I understand what's going on.
I won't die. I won't. ...Even if I have to claw and struggle, I'll survive.
I see. ...All right, then. Make sure you get up tomorrow morning. Eat your breakfast. And don't forget your bath and to brush your teeth.
Yeah, yeah. ...Later.
That was the end of the call.
...Sometimes my parents do go up to Tokyo for work meetings.
But Tokyo is far, after all. Normally they settle things over the phone...
And even when they do go, it's always planned in advance, never this sudden.
So I couldn't deny the nagging sense that something about this was off... unnatural.
At any rate... if I stick to the facts alone.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ...Tonight, I'm alone in this house.
When my parents come home from work, I'll be gone. Missing. Vanished.
...Thinking back on the chain of Oyashiro-sama's curses over the last five years, it wouldn't be strange at all.
Come to think of it... it's getting late.
Isn't it bad that the only light on is in my room upstairs?
I'd be practically telling the enemy my parents aren't home, handing them a chance.
First, I turn on the TV. I set the volume a little high.
Next, I run to Dad's study and turn on the light and music the same way.
This should make it look from outside like my parents are home.
I make another round of the house, checking the locks and looking for any gaps...
...When I looked at the balcony, I found the laundry still hanging out and went pale.
This makes it obvious! I have to bring it in, quick...!!
I hastily gather the laundry, erasing the evidence that Mom isn't here.
Maybe that's enough now... ...Ah! The garage!! My parents don't drive all the way to Tokyo, but they do drive to Okinomiya Station.
If the garage is left open with no car in it for everyone to see... that's bad!!
I dash outside in a panic and pull down the garage shutter, even though we never close it normally.
That should do it... a-ah, the newspaper slot!! Mom always brings the paper in. Since they left at noon... the evening edition is still sitting there!!
Just as I feared. I pull everything out of the mailbox and dump it all in the entryway.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ...Now... this time it should really be okay...
Come to think of it... that shoe cabinet I smashed up in my frenzy earlier... I can't just leave it like this.
Maybe I'll say I slipped and fell, and accidentally smashed it with the bat I was carrying...
Even so, I can't leave it like this. ...I'd better clean up at least a little before Mom tears into me.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: I remembered there was a broom and dustpan in the storage room and started to go get them, when the phone rang again deeper in the house.
Hello, Maebara speaking.

Oh my, Keiichi-kun?
Is your mother at home?
Ah, um, she isn't here right now...

You idiot, Maebara Keiichi!!
Don't go and blurt out that your parents aren't home...!!
You can still recover this, stay calm and handle it...!
I-I think she'll be back soon...!!
That comeback wasn't good either...!
At this rate, they'll just continue with I'll call again, or Please ask them to call me when they return...!!

Then that's quite all right.
It's not anything important...
Well then, excuse me.
It didn't turn into the kind of situation I feared, and I felt a rush of relief.
A small blessing in the midst of bad luck.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ......I still need to handle the calls for my parents tonight.
That call is over now... but ad-libbing this sweetly won't keep working.
While I still have time, I need to come up with a convincing lie that my parents are home but can't answer the phone.

They're frying tempura and can't step away.
...No, that's no good.

They're sick and bedridden.
......That might be a safe one?
As I thought it over and started to head back to my room, the phone rang out again.
It sounded almost as if they were calling because they already knew the lie I was about to tell.

I don't want to answer.
...But I have to.
They'll suspect no one's home.
......I should've left the receiver off the hook and said I hadn't noticed...
But this call ringing right now, at least this one, I have to answer.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: I brace myself and pick up the receiver.........
Hello......
I decide not to identify myself as Maebara either. There's no need to be polite to someone whose identity I don't even know...
But compared to my brusque voice, the caller's voice was absurdly cheerful.

Terribly sorry to call so late at night.
My name is Ooishi, from Okinomiya Shobo,
O-Ooishi-san?!
Maebara-san? Good evening. I'm glad to hear you sound well.
W-wait just a second!
...I switched to the cordless handset and ran back up to my room on the second floor.
With no one else in the house, it made no difference where I talked... but I still wanted to speak with Ooishi-san somewhere I felt even a little safer.
S-sorry to keep you waiting.
How have you been since then?
Since then? Since when, exactly?
...There was no mistaking it, his bland, innocent way of saying it rubbed me the wrong way.

The last time I spoke with Ooishi-san was... the day before yesterday, I guess. The day I stayed home from school, I ran into him on my way back from the hospital... we went into town for lunch, and talked...
...And after that, Rena and Mion came to visit and interrogated me.
Whenever I talk to Ooishi-san... somehow, they always find out.
Maybe that had been true ever since the first time I met him.
Would this call today be completely exposed to them too...?
Hello...? Can you hear me, Maebara-san?
Huh? Ah, sorry... Um, what did you say?
I asked whether anything has changed since then... You didn't answer, so I got worried.
Uh, well... not really...
I started to say it, then stopped.
There had been a mountain of changes. Every one of them was baffling.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: I had no idea how to explain any of it... but I'd ask anyway.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: If I didn't ask now, I might never get another chance.
...Because there was no guarantee I'd make it safely through tonight with my parents gone.
Um, Ooishi-san... I really do think... someone's trying to kill me.
Really?!
...It could've been a coincidence, but... the other day, when I was out sick, those two came to visit me that evening.
Those two?
Rena and Mion. ...And there, they pressed me about having lunch with you, Ooishi-san.
They left some ohagi as a get-well gift, but... there was a needle in one of them. ...By sheer luck, I didn't swallow it, but... Doesn't this make it a threat after all...?
And the needle?
...Uh, it looked like an ordinary sewing needle, the kind you see anywhere. It had an eye for thread...
No, Maebara-san, the needle itself. It can be evidence. We might be able to prove it was a threat. Do you still have that needle?
...R-right... that's it!!!!
I threw down the handset and bolted downstairs.
When I hurled that ohagi away, terror made me look away... but that needle was crucial evidence!
I'm sure of it... I threw it at the wall with the ohagi. If it's anywhere... it has to be that wall in the living room!
But my meticulous mother had cleaned the living room wall spotless, and every trace of the splattered ohagi was gone.
Could it have fallen into the gap between the wall and the carpet?!
I felt around roughly with my palm, but got no response.
I tried moving the table and sofa, pulling out the carpet, and beating it hard.
But I couldn't find the needle...!
Did Mom clean it up with the ohagi without noticing?
At most, it was only the day before yesterday. I don't know when burnable trash day is, but it might still be in the kitchen garbage bag!!
I rushed into the kitchen, opened the trash bin, and spread out its contents.
But... one glance told me that finding a needle in this tangled pile of garbage would be a herculean task.
Literally, it'd be like searching for a bead in the desert...
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: Right, I'll try smacking through it with my hands.
It's a little filthy, but I'm looking for a needle. If I feel even a tiny prick, I'll find it!
It might be rough, but it's the quickest way.
I held my breath, then slapped both hands down on the pile of trash over and over.
Filth splattered everywhere, disgusting beyond words, but now is no time to be saying that!!
...I kept repeating it for a while, but got no response.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: I want to search more thoroughly... but I'm still on the phone. I shouldn't keep Ooishi-san waiting too long...
When Mom gets back later, I'll have to ask her whether there was a needle.
I grabbed the red pen and scrawled roughly on the memo stuck to the fridge with a magnet: "Was there a needle?!"
Then, to get back to Ooishi-san's call after making him wait too long, I ran up the stairs.
Hello? How did it go?
...I couldn't find it... I was just too shaken at the time...
...I see. It's fine if you find it later. Please keep it safe.
Right, it wasn't just the needle. I should tell him about the hit-and-run this morning too.
Ah, also, Ooishi-san, that's not all. Actually, this morning...
...That car was definitely aiming for me. From all the circumstantial evidence, I can say that for certain.
Did you see that car's license plate? We'll look for it on our side too.
Damn it...!!
Back then I just yelled at him in a rage... I never looked at the plate...
But whether it's the needle or the plate, what a blunder!
I've been so obsessed with protecting myself that I've fallen short on what mattered most...!
Frustration and helplessness welling up, I slam my fist into the pillow.
S-sorry... other than it being a white wagon, I... don't know...
It can't be helped, Maebara-san. Anyone would panic if they were almost run over.
...So these really aren't coincidences, are they?
Ooishi-san began to grunt on the other end of the phone.
I could picture him standing there with his arms folded...
And... Rena still seems suspicious after all.
...How did she put it?
On the way home today, Rena said this to me. ...Why are you so much like Satoshi-kun?
Now I can say it with certainty.
Rena knows what happened to Satoshi.
...She knows more than that he simply disappeared...
...Rena... knows. ...She knows something about Satoshi, who was spirited away last year.
What exactly do you mean by that...?
...According to Rena... I apparently look just like Satoshi. If this keeps up... I'll follow the same fate as Satoshi... that's the kind of thing she says.
Fate, is it. ...Did she say specifically what kind of "fate" you'd follow?
...Um... "transferring schools"...
Transferring schools?
Rena says Satoshi "transferred schools." ...And that if I keep going like this, I'll "transfer" too...
Ooishi-san let out an especially grim sigh and gave a deep grunt.
...............Maebara-san, that is probably... some kind of threat... or perhaps a warning.
I think so too...
At that moment... I remember.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: I wonder whether it's really right to assume everything that's happened so far was done by a human culprit.
Besides the theory that Rena and the others are the culprits, there's still the theory that Oyashiro-sama's curse is real...
Of course, I can't tell Ooishi-san anything like that.
But... in both theories, one thing is the same: Rena is suspicious.
Whether Oyashiro-sama's curse is real, or it's some village-wide crime.
...Rena is involved.
...What does Rena know? Rena is suspicious. Who is Rena?
At least her connection to the string of mysterious deaths in the past is beyond doubt...
I'm pretty sure Ooishi-san said he'd looked into Rena a little...
When he said a little, that was probably just Ooishi-san's roundabout way of putting it.
...In other words, he's probably looked into it much more deeply than that.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ...I want to ask about Rena.
I want to know what happened at her old school... and the things I don't know.
Whether Rena is... someone to be suspected... no... that's not it.
...I want to know the truth.
Tonight, I'm alone in this big house.
...My parents, unreliable as they are, aren't here, and as long as they were home, I thought that meant I was safe.
This house isn't a fortress or a castle.
...If someone with malicious intent resorted to force, they'd get in easily.
There are no other houses around the Maebara home. No matter what noise was made, nobody would hear it.
I'd never resented my dad's artist pretensions of building a house in a backwater like this more than I did now...
...When morning comes tomorrow... will I still be here...?
So I'll ask. I'll ask now. Because I don't know whether I'll ever get another chance.
Um... Ooishi-san, there's something I want to ask you. ...Please don't hide anything from me.
Yes. Ask me anything.
Though he was as far away as could be, on the other end of the line, I'd never found anyone so reassuring.
I'll ask.
I'll ask about Rena.
...What happened at her old school...!
...Actually... about Rena,
I'd been hearing that sound for a while.
I was so absorbed in the phone call that I ignored it at first... but eventually I realized it was the doorbell.
The time was... seven.
It wasn't an hour for the mailman, and not an hour when neighbors come calling, either.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: I thought about pretending not to be home, but that would be bad.
It would ruin the whole act of having my family at home.
I had to answer.
...Hello? Maebara-san?
Ah, sorry, it sounds like someone's here, so I'm going to the door for a second.
A visitor? Sorry, sorry. Shall we end tonight's call here?
That won't do!
Ah, no, I'll be right back! Is it okay if we leave the line as it is?!
...All right, all right. I don't mind.
I tossed the receiver onto the futon and ran to the entryway.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: I had to smooth-talk them and send them away.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: Somehow, I felt it might be the same woman who had called just before Ooishi-san did.
...Then it was probably one of Mom's friends, or a neighbor.
Mom said she wasn't feeling well and went to bed early tonight... Yeah, that sounded safe enough.
No normal person would insist on waking someone who'd gone to bed sick.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: Just as before, the chime kept ringing at steady intervals.
...If no one answers after all this ringing, wouldn't most people give up and go home...?
Keeping the chain on, I cracked the door and peered at the visitor.
...A cold shiver crawled down my spine.
I knew.
...Somewhere in my heart, I'd been braced for this.
...I was running away, clinging to the safest guess I could make, that it was one of Mom's friends.
...Good evening.
Re-... Rena...
Rena shouldn't have any reason to come at this hour.
The timing was unsettling, too.
...It was right when I was about to ask Ooishi-san about Rena.
I wanted to write it off as mere coincidence.
But those creepy words Mion said to me here at the door the other day came back to me...
.........Well now... there isn't anything this old man doesn't know.
R-... Rena alone...?
...Yeah.
It seemed Mion wasn't with her. ...But that didn't change anything.
What did you come here for...?
...Hey, Keiichi-kun, I want to open the door and talk properly... Is Rena not allowed to come in through the front door? ...is she?
Certainly, talking through the chain wasn't how you treated a classmate. ...But...
At our place, we always put the chain on at night. Don't worry about it.
Rena lowered her head, looking terribly sad.
But only her lips were smiling, and the effort it took to hold that smile was painful to watch.
...But even as I clawed at the pain in my chest, I didn't let my guard down.
As long as I stayed like this, my heart might hurt, but at least my life wasn't in danger.

What I truly feared... more than simply removing this chain and having thugs rush in and attack me, was being attacked by Rena after trusting her enough to unhook it... and having that friendship betrayed.
As long as I didn't undo the chain, my heart would ache, but... at least I wouldn't be betrayed by Rena.
After silently pressing me and seeing no sign I'd remove the chain, Rena seemed to give up on forcing her way in through the front door.
...Hey, Keiichi-kun... have you eaten?
No, I hadn't.
With Mom not home, no matter how long I waited, dinner wasn't coming.
I'd lain down after getting back, then the phone woke me, and I'd been talking the whole time, so I never had the chance to eat.
...It'd be cup ramen anyway. If I wanted it, I could eat anytime.
...No... not yet. What's it to you?
A-ahahaha, then that's a relief☆ Look at this. I brought you some side dishes and stuff.
Saying that, Rena held out a tiered lunch box wrapped in a furoshiki.
I-if you let me use the kitchen, I can warm up the miso soup too☆
It's fine, you don't have to do that much...
B-but, it's full of tofu and vegetables! Keiichi-kun, you don't hate that kind of thing... do you? ...Do you?
No way I'd hate it.
I love miso soup loaded with ingredients.
Daikon, carrots, burdock, potatoes, that hearty texture and volume... ahh... that kind of miso soup is the best...
I brought rice too, so if you heat it in the microwave you can eat right away☆
Of course, rice is indispensable with miso soup! I shovel rice down by the mouthful, and sip miso soup in between... Ahh, thank heavens I was born Japanese...!!
And also, I made pickles again☆ This time they're wild mountain vegetable pickles!
Until I moved to Hinamizawa, I underestimated sansai, the bounty of the mountains.
But... one taste, and I was completely captivated by them!
Light, yet deeply flavorful.
Compared to these, those so-called greengrocer vegetables are coarse and no good!
If you ask me, those are vegetables for beginners.
You only understand what sansai really are once you reach my level of expertise.
And it's common knowledge that the Ryugu family's special pickles are unbelievably delicious!
...Ahh, what kind of pickles are they...?
They must go perfectly with fluffy white rice...!!
...And then, and then!
Ahh, there's still more...!!
It sounds delicious, truly delicious!!
Farewell to the unhealthy me who used to say cup ramen was good enough!
Rena, looking immensely cheerful, suggested a dinner that sounded absolutely delicious.
...My stomach suddenly lost all its tension and started crying out in hunger...
...And at the same time, the wariness I'd felt toward Rena began to fade...
She says she's by herself... maybe it wouldn't be so bad to let her in...
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: Though the suspicion that she might poison me... still wouldn't go away...
................At that moment, that crawling chill ran down my back again.
At that moment, I couldn't understand why I was suddenly feeling that way.
...But... another me inside my head... sounded the alarm.
...Rena's cheerful, enticing talk about dinner... was based on one premise.
That premise was... that there was no dinner at my house tonight... in other words, that Mom, the one who cooks, wasn't here.
In a normal household, around seven is right in the middle of dinner.
At our house too, if Mom's around, we eat at about that time.
Just the fact that she showed up at that hour with ingredients for dinner was already strange.
...She knows.
Rena... knows... that my parents aren't home?
But... it could still be a bluff.
...I'd disguised things in various ways, with the room lights and so on, to make it look like my parents were home.
...It's possible Rena herself doesn't have proof my parents are away...
...But... is it...? The laundry, the garage, the evening paper... There were plenty of traces I'd hurriedly cleaned up.
...It's hard to say Rena never had a chance to suspect my parents were gone...
But... there should be no reason to confess that from my side...
...I'll hold out to the very end...
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: For one thing, I've got the chain on.
As long as I don't undo this, Rena can't do anything to me...
Ah... I appreciate it, but... it looks like dinner will be ready in a little bit.
Eh...? ...Really? Really?
...I appreciate it, and I'm sorry, but... um...
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: I couldn't think of a good way to refuse, and my voice trailed off weakly...
But... I think it could at least count as a side dish. ...I think.
Sorry, but... we're covered. My mom makes a lot of side dishes. So...
Eh...? You have side dishes?
With an awkward smile, I evasively brushed Rena off, looking apologetic...
But... the feeling I was trying to forget by ignoring it... slowly, slowly crept up my spine.
I was talking as though my mom was setting out dinner right now... but Rena and I weren't on the same page.
Rena was speaking from one obvious assumption.
And she was assuming it was something I naturally understood too.
So Keiichi-kun can make side dishes too, huh. ...What did you make, I wonder?
...N-no, it's... not like I...
Rena abruptly assumed those side dishes were something I made.
No... more than that, she was flatly asserting that my mom hadn't made dinner at all.
...Did you really make it? The side dish. You did, Keiichi-kun?
T-that's what I'm saying... I didn't make it. Mom made it... no, she's making it. Right now!
So... sorry, but I can't eat what you brought me...
At that, Rena fell silent. In that moment, I felt as if a shadow slid over her eyes.
...Keiichi-kun, want me to guess your dinner?
W-... what does it matter...?
...Let's see...
The conversation looked natural on the surface, but Rena had already taken control.
...And maybe it was my imagination, but the flow of it felt like I was being interrogated.
...That dinner... couldn't it be made with just hot water...?
H-hey, hey, don't underestimate it, okay?! Don't look down on my mom's special dinner...!! It's like a full imperial feast... so incredible it's ridiculous...,
I put on as brave a front as I can, but my molars won't stop clacking.
...If anything, it only makes me look even more shaken...
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: But Rena didn't react at all, even to that teasing.
...Keiichi-kun.... Did your mom really make your dinner...?
No, I'm telling you... she didn't leave it, she's making it right now! Dinner's almost ready...!
Rena completely ignores the premise I'm clinging to: that my mom is home right now, making dinner.
The more panicked I get... the colder Rena becomes.
Hey, Keiichi-kun.
...At that moment, an unnaturally cold draft seeped through the crack in the door...
Is your mother home? ............Is she?
I can't play dumb anymore.
Rena came here absolutely certain my parents weren't home.
But... I can't admit that now.
No matter what... my parents are home and dinner will be ready soon... that's how it is...!!
So I answer.
I answer that my mom is here.
S-She is... of course...!
I can feel the air drying out.
...Rena's eyes grow colder and colder, skewering me with a frozen stare...
...Why?
Huh, w-w-what do you mean...?
I meant it as a slightly playful dodge... but that paper-thin act is blown away the instant I meet Rena's eyes.
That gaze... told me Rena's answer before she even opened her mouth...
...Why have you been lying this whole time, I wonder...? ...I wonder.
...N-no... It's not a lie...
...It's a lie, isn't it?
...It's not a lie...
It's a lie!!!!
Rena's shout sent a jolt through my entire body...!!
With the chain still protecting me, the only point of contact between Rena and me was the narrow gap in the door, barely a dozen centimeters.
And yet, I was being cornered.
Rather than being sheltered by the safe zone called home, it felt more like a dead-end alley where no one would come to help...
...I'll guess your dinner, Keiichi-kun. Umm...
I realized Rena knew my parents weren't home tonight.
...But at this point, it got strange again...!
Even if she'd somehow learned my parents were out... there was no way she could guess what I was about to eat tonight...!!
But Rena said she could guess... How could she possibly know?!
How could she know that...?!
No... for a guy who can't cook, cup ramen is the obvious choice.
...Statistically, that was bound to be the most likely answer.
...That doesn't count as a real guess...!!!
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ...Ramen alone will probably leave you hungry. I think it'd keep you full longer if you had some rice with it too.
Calm down, Maebara Keiichi...
This is a coincidence.
...Lately, I've been treating Rena as someone special.
changeReason: Old says 'Rena was just reading certain tells of mine' which is completely different from JPN このところ俺はレナをある種の特別視している (I've been treating Rena as somehow special). The old describes Rena observing the narrator; the JPN describes the narrator viewing Rena differently.

TextENGNew: ...That's why even the slightest thing she sees through makes me panic this badly.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: But... it's not like Rena can read my mind.
If she really could read that, she'd be a yokai.
...Not that she's some yokai... There's no way. No way at all.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ...Do you like it?
Ramen... you mean?
Mm-mm, no--
Rena first told me my answer was off the mark.
Her denial was so brief that for a while, I couldn't grasp what her next words meant.
...S-sorry, Rena. ...What did you just say?
Huh? What?
Just now... you asked me, what do I like?
A few moments later, I regretted how carelessly I'd urged her to go on.
It was exactly because her answer was so simple that... I couldn't understand what it meant.
...Tonkotsu ginger flavor.
Until that pure white blank in my head cleared, I wonder what kind of look I had on my face...
My vision warped... slowly spiraling counterclockwise... throwing my sense of balance into chaos...
How can you know that much?!!
I didn't deny it anymore. This was a kind of blind rage.
...How can Rena... know even that?!
I pressed toward Rena, not caring if my face slammed into the door.
...But Rena wasn't the least bit cowed by how I was acting.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: Yeah, I bought it. I bought a bunch. I bought a whole box! How do you know that?!
...Why do you think? Strange, isn't it? Isn't it?
Why dodge the question at a time like this?!
By now, the door chain wasn't there just to protect me anymore.
How do you know?! Why do you know?! Answer me!!!!
...You bought it at Seven Mart... right?
A chill crawls up my spine...
I cover it up with nothing but surface anger.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: Then... how do you know that?!?!
...Because I was right behind you the whole time, Keiichi-kun.
W-wh... what are you talking about...?!
I don't understand what she means by "stuck to."

That's what I mean.
Rena was.
Behind.
Keiichi-kun.
Stuck there the whole time.
...Ufufufu.
Like that night...?
When I was absorbed in my phone call with Ooishi-san, without letting me sense even a trace... beyond the door, right behind me... as if she'd stood there the entire time...?!?!
I watched from behind the whole time while Keiichi-kun was picking out all those ramen cups.

You were choosing all kinds, right.
Then your mother scolded you.
She told you to pick just one kind, because you kept choosing only expensive ramen.
And then Keiichi-kun picked his favorite, the big cup of tonkotsu ginger flavor☆@k@vS01/02/120200322.Rena likes tonkotsu ramen too.@k@vS01/02/120200323.But she can't finish the big cups.
My brain prickles numb, dulling my senses.
Maybe it's a defensive instinct, trying to thin out the fear and shield my mind.
The feeling of fear fades, and what Rena is saying now...
And what it means... scatters and vanishes from my mind...
changeReason: Old says the narrator began to understand Rena's words; JPN 脳内から雲散霧消させる means the meaning 'scatters and vanishes from the mind' — the opposite of comprehension.

TextENGNew: But that doesn't make the fear disappear.
...If I had to put it into words... it's like standing at the edge of a cliff and shutting your eyes so you don't look down; it solves nothing at all.
I reel and take one step back.@k...And for the distance I give up, Rena slips in.

.........So, Keiichi-kun.
................Open this, okay?
Let's eat together with Rena☆
I'm sure it'll be delicious, ................right...?
Rena's too-white, too-thin fingers, several of them, each seeming alive on its own, slide through the crack in the door and make the chain clatter...
If she'd just torn the chain off, maybe I could have let the feeling called fear explode honestly.
changeReason: Old says 'unlatched the chain' (calm action) while JPN 引きちぎってくれれば means 'torn the chain off' (violent action); different implied intensity.

TextENGNew: ...But Rena doesn't do that.
She insists on making me open the chain myself.
Inside my chest... I frantically try to set my damp gunpowder alight with a lighter. I try over and over. Click, click. ...But it won't catch. ...It won't catch...!
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ......Open up?　......Keiichi-kun...?
G...go home... Please... just go home...!!
......Why would you say something so mean, I wonder...? ...I wonder?
Go home!!　Get out!!!　Get ouuuuut!!!!
At last, the gunpowder inside me catches.
It doesn't smolder; it explodes all at once.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: I hurl myself against the door.
It seemed the impact through the door made Rena lose her footing a little.
I can't hesitate here...!!
I grab the doorknob with both hands, brace with both feet, and yank with all my strength!!
But there was no light, brisk slam.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: Instead, a grinding, awful resistance came through, as the door refused to close.
They were... Rena's fingers.
Each finger writhes, wavering in the gap of the door like the tendrils of a carnivorous plant...!
......It hurts... it hurts, Keiichi-kun... it hurts... ngh,
It wasn't a violent scream, but a quiet groan, as if she were swallowing it down.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: Go home... go home... go home...!!!
I pull the door tighter and tighter...!!
It never occurred to me that unless I loosened the door once, Rena's fingers couldn't come free, and that meant I couldn't shut it.
...It really hurts, Keiichi-kun.　...If I took the joke too far, I'll apologize... uu...
I didn't care about her words of apology.
No matter how much Rena apologized, nothing about what she'd done would change.　...Nothing would change!
...It hurts... it hurts... I'm sorry... I'm sorry... I'm sorry...
Go home, go home!!　Get ouuuut!!
Even if Rena wanted to go home, she couldn't, because I had her fingers caught in the door...
Rena's white fingertips had turned bright red, and no longer so much as twitched.
I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry...
Go home, go home, go home...!!!
I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry,
Rena's apologies, sometimes twisted with pain... like a broken tape recorder... did nothing but repeat those same words over and over...
Go home go home go home... stop it stop it stop it!!!
I pulled the door even harder shut!!!
Then, in an instant, by some chance, Rena's fingers slipped free of the door's bite.
The moment they did, the door slammed shut with force, and from the other side I heard Rena fall to the floor.
Without a moment's pause, I locked it.
It rang out with a loud clack, pronouncing my rejection to Rena.
I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry... Keiichi-kun... I'm sorry... open this... Keiichi-kun...
Leaning limply against the door, Rena did nothing but repeat words of apology.
As I slowly confirmed that I'd completely sealed off the space between Rena and me... I edged away from the door, step by step.
From beyond the door, Rena's words of apology... those pitiful words, I'm sorry, I'm sorry... kept repeating forever, until she could win my forgiveness.
I didn't feel sorry for her.
But it wasn't out of cruelty that I felt that way.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ...There was only a gray sense of relief that I'd managed to escape Rena.
Before, at this very front door, Mion threatened me, saying there was nothing she didn't know...
And now, in this same place again, Rena taught me the same thing.
...My little pretense that my parents weren't home had been useless from the very start.
...In that case, I'd have been better off pretending not to be home and never opening the door!
My petty little schemes... are good for nothing.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: Here in Hinamizawa... there's no way... I can ever outwit them...!!!
Even through a door, I wanted to put as much distance as I could between myself and Rena.
Step by step, as I moved farther away, Rena's sobbing apologies faded with me...
I raced up the stairs in one burst and hurled myself into my room.
...At least now, I couldn't hear Rena's endlessly repeated apologies anymore.
I threw myself into the futon and recoiled at the hard, grating feel under me...
There's... something in the futon?!?!
...It was the phone receiver.
Only then did I remember... I was in the middle of a phone call with Ooishi-san.
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: When I looked at the clock, I realized almost no time had passed since I'd gone down to the front door.
...Is my clock battery dying...??
After talking with Rena that much... there's no way so little time could have passed...!!
But the second hand kept moving, as if nothing were strange at all, ticking off each second exactly one by one...
When I put the receiver, still faintly warm from my hand, back to my ear... frozen time slowly began to thaw.
...Hello... Ooishi-san? Sorry to keep you waiting.
No, no. I haven't been waiting that long.
It was obvious that time was passing differently for Ooishi-san and me.
The lively voices leaking through from what sounded like a sports program on his end told me how far away Ooishi-san felt...
...Rena just came over.
Did she come to hang out?
I had no confidence I could explain this situation to Ooishi-san accurately.
But what I needed right now wasn't to explain that. It was Rena.
Right. Rena's visit had interrupted us... but I'd been about to ask Ooishi-san about Rena.
What was truth and what was lies... I couldn't tell at all.
The only thing I knew was one stark fact: Rena was suspicious.
If I asked Ooishi-san about who Rena really was, I might learn something.
...Up to now, I kept forcing myself to ask about things I was better off not knowing, and I regretted it over and over.
But in that sense, you could say I'd already sunk to the very bottom.
There was no way there could be any regret worse than this.
No... if there was anything beyond even this, I wanted to know that too.
Now I was in a situation where anything could happen, not tomorrow, but tonight.
I wanted to know everything I wanted to know.
...I absolutely couldn't die like this.
...Not like this, while I still understand nothing... never...!
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: ...You mean Ryuuguu Rena-san? I looked into her. Well... not exactly lightly.
I knew Ooishi-san's way of speaking. Not exactly lightly meant he'd investigated enough that it was hard to tell me, as her friend.
I want to hear all of that not exactly lightly.
...There isn't anything there you'd enjoy hearing, you know?
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: Ooishi-san.
In as calm a voice as I could manage, I spoke to Ooishi-san as he kept dodging the point.
And then... I said it outright.
I think Ryuuguu Rena is suspicious. Even if the past incidents were truly Oyashiro-sama's curse... Ryuuguu Rena is involved.
Do you have concrete evidence that makes you think Rena-san is suspicious?
Ooishi-san's tone turned sharply severe.
...Do I have evidence?
Those were the words of a detective.
Only circumstantial evidence.
Even through the receiver, I could tell Ooishi-san was disappointed.
It felt like he'd gotten a bite, reeled in, found no change at all in the bait called me... and then, disappointed, cast the rod again.
...Ooishi-san. If there isn't evidence you can see... you can't act?
Behind those words, I laced in another thorn: If there's no evidence, you can't save me?
As expected of Ooishi-san, who prefers speaking in circles, he seemed to understand what I was really trying to say.
It's all right, Maebara-san. I'll protect you.
...Not reassuring in the slightest.
Ooishi-san was only using me for his investigation.
Even if I got killed, he'd probably think of my corpse as nothing more than valuable evidence.
Your investigation may have nothing to do with whether I live or die, but for me, if I die, that's the end!
Ooishi-san fell silent on the other end of the line.
...I said it in a burst of emotion, but I didn't care.
...As long as Ooishi-san understood how desperate my situation was, that was enough.

So please, tell me.
About Rena.

Satoshi transferred schools.
...And in the not-so-distant future, I'll probably 'transfer' too, just like Rena says.
...But I doubt even you will be able to find my body, Ooishi-san.
...You still haven't even found Satoshi's body!!
M-Maebara-san, please calm down...
Even without Ooishi-san trying to calm me, I forced myself to rein in my agitation...
Ranting about my distrust of the police here wouldn't solve anything.
In the end, the only things that can protect me are myself... and the metal bat Satoshi left behind.

Then at least tell me.
What happened to Rena before she transferred.
...You're prepared for this to be a dull story, yes?
changeReason: New translation contains substantially different content from the original.

TextENGNew: After realizing my resolve wouldn't waver, Ooishi-san finally gave in.

Right now, there isn't a single dull story for me.
...Please.
...First, there are a few things I need to say in advance.
Yes.
Please keep this confidential. Also, some of what I'll tell you may include speculation. ...Not all of it may be true. ...Is that all right with you?
It might not be true...? I don't really understand what you mean.

There is no central investigation headquarters for the series of mysterious deaths in Hinamizawa. Each year's incident is handled separately. Therefore, Ryugu Rena has never once come up as a suspect. ...In other words,
...The investigation into Rena wasn't done by the police... but as a personal inquiry, is what you're saying?
...I appreciate your understanding. ...Most of what I have comes from phone calls, or from meeting people and hearing what they had to say.
So none of it has been corroborated. I'm asking you not to take it all at face value... that's what I mean. You understand, yes?
...All of it is just from what you heard?
Yes, I'm sorry. It's a personal investigation, you see.
Wait... Didn't you say before that you'd seen Rena's medical chart? I'm sure that's what you told me.
Ooishi-san paused for a brief moment on the other end of the line.
...Did I say that? Ahaha! Please pretend you never heard that.
I had no interest in the adult complications Ooishi-san had to deal with.
Besides, proof or no proof, it didn't matter.
Even if it was all just rumors, where there's smoke, there's fire.
...Please tell me. Ooishi-san.
Understood...
Ooishi-san finally opened his heavy lips.
It seemed Rena had lived in Hinamizawa a long time ago.
When it came time for elementary school, she moved far away, to Ibaraki.
And then... eventually. ...Right before she transferred back.
She went around smashing every window in her school.
And then, at the doctor's office, Rena confessed.
"It was Oyashiro-sama," she said.
That was everything I knew.
...What I know isn't much different from what you've told me, Maebara-san.
Then what exactly did you investigate further...?
I didn't even need to ask.
...It was the incident right before she transferred.
The incident Rena caused... and what she confessed to the doctor afterward... right?
Ooishi-san, you suspect Rena and the others precisely because you know the details of that, don't you?
...........Yes. I do suspect them.
So Rena and the others really are the culprits...?
Ah, no... that's not what I mean by suspect.
Ooishi-san was the type to speak with considerable confidence, but these words alone felt terribly unreliable.
Then what... do you suspect?
...............Oyashiro-sama... is what I suspect.
Huh?
...Oyashiro-sama's curse... could it really exist...? Well, hahaha...!
Ooishi-san's laugh was dry, the kind you couldn't possibly join in on.
Ooishi-san began to speak, little by little.
About the suspicions he'd felt since around the time of Satoshi's disappearance.
...About the chain of events that led him to investigate Rena's past.
Just then, with a rumble of distant thunder, a sudden downpour began.
It came without any warning, a fierce rain that hammered the earth.
The window I'd left cracked open to let out the room's heat let in a gust of wild wind, making the curtains flap noisily.
What is it?
Ah, nothing, it just started raining really hard out of nowhere... Sorry, please continue...
I stood up while keeping the phone going and reached for the window.
I said at the start that it was an incident, but since neither the school nor the victims ever pressed charges, it never became an official case.
...And you see, this is where everyone involved clams up. ...Even though one of the victims was beaten so badly that one eye suffered permanent damage.

It could be the school, or it could be someone who didn't want this going public, who pulled strings behind the scenes. ...Also, the neurologist who handled the counseling was very strict about professional ethics...
...Hello? Maebara-san? Can you hear me?
By the streetlight near the gate's mailbox, a single figure had been standing there the whole time.
In this downpour, without an umbrella. Drenched from head to toe, of course... Water dripped from her hair in the rain that fell like a waterfall...
Her arms hung limply at her sides; the only word for it was that she was standing there, motionless.
In one hand... was the tiered lunch box, wrapped in furoshiki.
Her eyes were fixed on... my room. ...On me, as I pulled back the curtain to close the window.
Her mouth... kept moving... in a steady, rhythmic pattern.
As though she were chewing on something impossibly tough, her mouth stuffed full.
What... is she eating... standing there like that...?
Why was it that at this moment... I was more transfixed by Rena... than by the shocking revelations Ooishi-san was sharing with me...?
If it hadn't rained, I never would have gone near the window.
Then I would never have noticed Rena, nor would I have noticed... that...
Rena's mouth was repeating the same motion, over and over.
She wasn't eating something... she was repeating something.
What?
Was she speaking?
To me...?
What was she saying...?
Why was I... pressed right up against the glass... straining my eyes to make it out?
Hello? Maebara-san? Can you hear me? Hellooo...
I'm sorry.
Huh? Maebara-san?
I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry...
Hello? Maebara-san?! What happened...?!
Rena... in this torrential downpour... was still apologizing, over and over...............
The other me inside me swung my right arm and drew the curtain shut, blocking out the outside world.
But something like that wouldn't stop Rena's endlessly repeating apology from reaching my ears.
If I forgave her, would that mean she'd forgive me?!
Stop it, stop it, stop it, stop it...!
I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry, I'm sorry...
Damn it...!! Why should I... have to forgive her?!
If anything, I'm the one who needs forgiving!
What part of me is so unforgivable?!
I absolutely refuse to be killed!!
If I can't be forgiven, then I won't forgive either.
...I won't forgive, I won't forgive, I won't forgive, I won't forgive, I won't forgive, I won't forgive, I won't forgive, I won't forgive...!!
Maebara-san? If you can hear me, please respond. ...Hellooo!!
